questions.q: Rajendra Chola I was the son of which great Chola emperor?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I was the son and successor of Rajaraja Chola I.

questions.q: In which century did Rajendra Chola I lead the Chola empire to its greatest extent?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Chola I reigned in the 11th century CE, around 1014 to 1044.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I famously led a military expedition to which great northern river?
questions.explanation: Rajendra's army marched north to the Ganges, earning him fame as 'Lord of the Ganges.'

questions.q: What new capital city did Rajendra Chola I found?
questions.explanation: Rajendra built a grand new capital called Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I is especially remembered for naval campaigns to which region?
questions.explanation: Rajendra launched a major naval expedition across the seas to Southeast Asia.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Rajendra was a ruler of the Chola dynasty of the Tamil country.

questions.q: The maritime power he overran in Southeast Asia was the empire of which name?
questions.explanation: Rajendra's fleet attacked the Srivijaya empire, based in Sumatra and the Malay region.

questions.q: The name of Rajendra's new capital, Gangaikonda Cholapuram, refers to which feat?
options: Bringing Ganges water after his northern conquest
questions.explanation: The name means 'the city of the Chola who conquered the Ganges,' commemorating his northern campaign.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I continued his father's policy by maintaining Chola control over which island?
questions.explanation: Rajendra retained and tightened Chola rule over Sri Lanka, begun by his father.

questions.q: Like his father, Rajendra built a great temple. Where is his temple located?
questions.explanation: Rajendra built the Gangaikondacholisvaram temple at his new capital.

questions.q: Which famous title did Rajendra take to commemorate his march to the Ganges?
questions.explanation: He adopted the title Gangaikonda Chola, 'the Chola who conquered the Ganges.'

questions.q: Whom did Rajendra's forces defeat during the northern Ganges expedition?
questions.explanation: Rajendra's general defeated the Pala king Mahipala I in Bengal during the Ganges campaign.

questions.q: Roughly what years are assigned to Rajendra Chola I's reign?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I reigned approximately from 1014 CE to 1044 CE.

questions.q: The Chola naval expedition to Southeast Asia under Rajendra is considered remarkable because it was primarily what?
options: A trade embassy with no fighting
A rare large-scale overseas military campaign by an Indian power
A scientific survey of the seas
questions.explanation: It stands out as one of the few major overseas military expeditions launched by an Indian kingdom.

questions.q: Rajendra's Southeast Asian campaign is often linked to disputes over which lucrative interest?
options: Maritime trade with China and the sea routes
Pearl fisheries of the Persian Gulf
questions.explanation: The expedition is widely connected to securing the lucrative maritime trade routes to China through Srivijaya-controlled straits.

questions.q: Rajendra was associated with the throne as co-regent by his father in roughly which year?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I made Rajendra co-regent around 1012 CE before his own death.

questions.q: Which Western Chalukya rival did Rajendra Chola I repeatedly clash with over the Deccan frontier?
options: Jayasimha II / the Chalukyas of Kalyani
questions.explanation: Rajendra fought the Western (Kalyani) Chalukyas, including their ruler Jayasimha II, over the Tungabhadra frontier.

questions.q: The temple Rajendra built at his new capital was modelled closely on which earlier temple?
questions.explanation: Gangaikondacholisvaram was modelled on his father's Brihadisvara temple, though more refined in form.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I is sometimes nicknamed by modern writers as the Indian equivalent of which European conqueror, echoing the epithet given to Samudragupta?
questions.explanation: Owing to his wide conquests, Rajendra (like Samudragupta) is sometimes dubbed an 'Indian Napoleon.'

questions.q: At Gangaikonda Cholapuram, Rajendra had a great water reservoir built and called it by what name, recalling his Ganges triumph?
questions.explanation: He created a vast tank named Chola Gangam, filled symbolically with Ganges water, as a 'liquid pillar of victory.'

questions.q: Who immediately succeeded Rajendra Chola I on the Chola throne?
questions.explanation: Rajendra was succeeded by his son Rajadhiraja I.

questions.q: Rajendra's overseas reach extended Chola influence over parts of which present-day archipelago nations?
questions.explanation: His campaign struck Srivijaya holdings across the regions of modern Indonesia and Malaysia.

questions.q: Which Srivijaya capital city is named in Chola inscriptions as a target of Rajendra's naval raid?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions list Kadaram (Kedah) and Srivijaya among the places struck by Rajendra's fleet.

questions.q: Rajendra's general who led the Ganges expedition carried Ganges water back so the king could do what?
options: Consecrate his new capital's tank with it
questions.explanation: The sacred water was used to fill the Chola Gangam tank, sanctifying the new capital.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I sent diplomatic embassies to which contemporary East Asian imperial court?
questions.explanation: Rajendra dispatched embassies to the Song court of China to foster trade relations.

questions.q: The Tiruvalangadu copper plates are an important inscriptional source dating from the reign of which ruler?
questions.explanation: The Tiruvalangadu copper-plate grant was issued in Rajendra Chola I's reign and details Chola genealogy and conquests.

questions.q: Rajendra placed which member of his family in charge of the Pandya and Chera country as a viceroy bearing a special title?
questions.explanation: Rajendra appointed viceroys styled 'Chola-Pandya' to govern the southern Pandya and Chera territories.

questions.q: The temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: Like the Thanjavur temple, Gangaikondacholisvaram is a Shiva temple.

questions.q: Rajendra's capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram remained the Chola capital for roughly how long after its founding?
questions.explanation: Gangaikonda Cholapuram served as the Chola capital for around two and a half centuries before declining.

questions.q: Rajendra Chola I's long Deccan frontier wars were fought chiefly against which rival power (a struggle that continued under his sons)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra's protracted Deccan struggle was chiefly against the Western Chalukyas of Kalyani, contested along the Tungabhadra; pitched battles such as Koppam followed under his sons.
